commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds stale auto-fallback-origin detection and deferred cleanup across reply and direct agent-command model selection, with regression coverage for session override cleanup.

PR surface: Source +288, Tests +315. Total +603 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue is source-reproducible on current main, and the remaining PR finding is source-reproducible by following the direct cleanup write after a stale row changes to another stale auto-fallback pin.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow branch repair can replace the direct path's broad stale predicate with an exact captured stale-pin guard and add the missing regression.

target.title: Fix stale auto-fallback origin model selection
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a shared stale auto-fallback-origin classifier so reply and agent-command selection agree when recorded fallback origin no longer matches the current primary.
- Defers reply-side model/auth cleanup until after read-only slash/inline actions have dispatched and a real run is about to start.
- Guards deferred cleanup against post-selection session changes so mixed `/model ...` directives and concurrent row updates are not overwritten.
- Prepares agent-command attempts with stale fallback model/auth cleared in memory, then persists durable cleanup only after a successful visible run and only if the store row still matches the stale fallback pin.

## Maintainer feedback addressed
- One prepared selection result now carries reset reason and persistence behavior through the reply turn.
- Stale-origin repair no longer emits the disallowed-allowlist reset event.
- Preserve-state/non-mutating agent-command runs strip fallback auth for the primary attempt without writing durable cleanup.
- Durable cleanup is guarded against concurrent user model switches and mixed inline model directives.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: stale auto-fallback model pins whose recorded fallback origin no longer matches the current primary were being treated as active stored overrides; the fix prepares the primary selection for the next real run and defers durable cleanup until the run boundary without overwriting user-owned model changes made after selection.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/92776-prepared-model-selection-boundary`, commit `a439e64438`, running the patched runtime module through Node 24 with `tsx` from the repository dependencies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the repository root and imported `createModelSelectionState` plus `applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry` from the patched source. The command exercised an unchanged stale auto-fallback row and a stale row changed by a simulated mixed `/model openai/gpt-4o hello` directive before deferred cleanup.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output from the live Node command:

```json
{
  "proof": "deferred-stale-auto-fallback-cleanup-guard",
  "head": "a439e64438",
  "unchangedStalePin": {
    "preparedSelection": "openai/gpt-4.1-mini",
    "cleanupPersisted": true,
    "afterCleanup": {
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackOriginProvider": null,
      "fallbackOriginModel": null,
      "authProfileOverride": null,
      "authProfileOverrideSource": null
    }
  },
  "mixedModelDirectiveBeforeRun": {
    "simulatedDirective": "/model openai/gpt-4o hello",
    "cleanupPersisted": false,
    "afterCleanup": {
      "providerOverride": "openai",
      "modelOverride": "gpt-4o",
      "modelOverrideSource": "user",
      "fallbackOriginProvider": null,
      "fallbackOriginModel": null,
      "authProfileOverride": null,
      "authProfileOverrideSource": null
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the prepared reply selection uses the current primary and cleans stale fallback state when the row is unchanged, but deferred cleanup fails closed after a mixed `/model` directive changes the row, preserving the user-selected `openai/gpt-4o` override.
- What was not tested: no live external provider API call was sent; this proof exercises the local OpenClaw model-selection runtime and session mutation boundary.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof uses a redacted throwaway session entry to avoid publishing real auth profile names.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper review on this PR identified that the previous deferred cleanup call could erase a mixed `/model ...` plus real prompt selection before the run.

## Verification
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core
- pnpm tsgo:test:src
- pnpm lint:core
- CI=1 pnpm check:changed -- --base upstream/main --head HEAD
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts

Fixes #92776
Supersedes #92790
